content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed a single amendment claiming the branch fails the locked test gate. Verified independently: the branch's only code change is a 4-line doc comment in persist_next_step_hint. The pre_commit_failure_remediation_survives_resume test fails on both this branch and on master (the merge base 313bb92e), confirming the regression is pre-existing and not introduced by this branch. The clean_timeout_teardown_yields_timeout_without_warning test passed in isolation across three runs on this branch. A comment-only change cannot affect either test's behavior, and the amendment's remediation hint ('rebase onto a green baseline') is not actionable because master is not green for the cited test.

## Votes

- `fr-1-f68e9794`: Reject
  The amendment misattributes a pre-existing master-level regression to this branch. I reproduced the pre_commit_failure_remediation_survives_resume failure on master at commit 313bb92e (the merge base) with the identical error ('remediation exhausted at cycle 4; maximum supported cycles is 3'), so no baseline rebase can make the gate green. The branch's only code change is a 4-line comment in persist_next_step_hint in src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs — that function is wholly unrelated to pre-commit remediation or timeout classification, and a comment cannot cause either test failure. The clean_timeout_teardown_yields_timeout_without_warning test passed cleanly in isolation across three consecutive runs on this branch; any observed failure is flakiness under parallel load, not something this doc change could have introduced. The remediation work proposed ('fix the timeout-classification and resume-after-pre-commit regressions') is legitimately needed, but it belongs on separate beads/PRs targeting master, not as a blocker tacked onto a minimal documentation-only change. Accepting this amendment would block a correct change for reasons unrelated to the change itself.
